exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.notes: Top set, get comfortable with heavier loads while keeping perfect technique

exercises.name: Front squat [or box squat]
exercises.notes: If you low bar squat, do front squat. If you high bar squat, do barbell box squat

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Top set, get comfortable with heavier loads while keeping perfect technique

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Submaximal bench press, be hypercritical of form

exercises.name: Weighted pull-up
exercises.notes: 1.5x shoulder width grip, pull your chest to the bar

exercises.name: Glute-ham raise [or Nordic ham curl]
exercises.notes: Keep your hips straight, do Nordic ham curls if no GHR machine

exercises.name: Seated face pull
exercises.notes: Don't go too heavy, focus on mind-muscle connection

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.notes: Technique work, avoid turning these into touch-and-go reps

exercises.name: Barbell overhead press
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes to keep your torso upright, press up and slightly back

exercises.name: Bulgarian split squat
exercises.notes: Start with your weaker leg working. Squat deep

exercises.name: Meadows row
exercises.notes: Brace with your other hand, stay light, emphasize form

exercises.name: Barbell or EZ bar curl
exercises.notes: Use minimal momentum, control the eccentric phase

exercises.name: Pec flye
exercises.notes: Perform with cables, bands, or dumbbells. Use full ROM. Stretch your pecs at the bottom

exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.notes: Sit back and down, keep your upper back tight to the bar

exercises.name: Pin squat
exercises.notes: Set the pins to around parallel. Dead stop on the pins, don't bounce and go

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Working top set, build confidence with heavier loads

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Focus on perfecting technique, slight pause on the chest

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Try to stay fluid with these, think of them as "pause-and-go"

exercises.name: Chin-up
exercises.notes: As many reps as possible, but stop at RPE8

exercises.name: Single-leg hip thrust
exercises.notes: Keep your chin tucked down and squeeze your glutes to move the weight

exercises.name: Cable reverse flye
exercises.notes: Keep elbows locked in place, squeeze the cable handles hard!

exercises.name: Standing calf raise
exercises.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep, full squeeze at the top

exercises.name: 6" Block pull
exercises.notes: Get very tight prior to pulling, use 85% if you're not experienced with block pulls. Use standard Deadlift 1RM for 1RM %s.

exercises.name: Pause db incline press
exercises.notes: 3-second pause. Sink the dumbbells as low as you comfortably can

exercises.name: Leg curl (choice)
exercises.notes: Use seated leg curl if available. Can use lying leg curl or Nordic ham curl if no machine access. Focus on the mind-muscle connection

exercises.name: Chest-supported row
exercises.notes: Can use machine or dumbbells. Full stretch at the bottom, squeeze at the top

exercises.name: Rope overhead triceps extension
exercises.notes: Focus on stretching the triceps at the bottom

exercises.name: Egyptian lateral raise
exercises.notes: Lean away from the cable. Focus on squeezing your delts.

exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.notes: Submaximal sets, approach these sets with confidence. Focus on technique

exercises.name: Barbell RDL
exercises.notes: Emphasize the stretch in your hamstrings, prevent your lower back from rounding

exercises.name: Unilateral leg press
exercises.notes: High and wide foot positioning, start with your weaker leg

exercises.name: Eccentric-accentuated leg extension
exercises.notes: 4-second lowering phase

exercises.name: Leg curl (choice)
exercises.notes: Dropset: do 10 reps, lower the weight ~30-50%, do another 15 reps. Use seated leg curl if available. Can use lying leg curl or Nordic ham curl if no machine access. Focus on the mind-muscle connection

exercises.name: Standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Think about rolling back and forth on the balls of your feet

exercises.name: A1: Weighted crunch
exercises.notes: Hold a plate or DB to your chest and crunch hard!

exercises.name: A2: Long-lever plank (Optional)
exercises.notes: Contract your glutes and position your elbows under your eyes to make the plank more difficult

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Submaximal sets, focus on form

exercises.name: Weighted pull-up
exercises.notes: 1.5x shoulder width grip, pull your chest to the bar

exercises.name: Machine incline press
exercises.notes: Smith machine or hammer strength machine. Use incline DB press if no machine access. ~45 degree incline.

exercises.name: Seated cable row
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together, drive your elbows down and back

exercises.name: Egyptian lateral raise
exercises.notes: Do 8-10 reps to failure. Rest 3-4 seconds. Do another 4 reps. Rest 2-3 seconds. Do another 4 reps.

exercises.name: Constant-tension cable triceps kickback
exercises.notes: Maintain a consistent pace of 1 second up and 1 second down

exercises.name: Hammer "cheat" curl
exercises.notes: You can use slight momentum on the concentric, but control the eccentric with your elbows stationary

exercises.name: Reset deadlift
exercises.notes: Stand up between each rep to work on technqiue

exercises.name: Hack squat
exercises.notes: Allow your knees to come forward (past your toes), focus the tension on your quads

exercises.name: Single-leg hip thrust
exercises.notes: Contract your glutes hard at the top

exercises.name: A1: Glute-ham raise [or Nordic ham curl]
exercises.notes: Keep your hips straight, do Nordic ham curls if no GHR machine

exercises.name: A2: Prisoner back extension
exercises.notes: Place your hands behind your head and squeeze your glutes to straighen your hips

exercises.name: Unilateral standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Start with your weaker side. Think about rolling back and forth on the balls of your feet

exercises.name: L-sit hold
exercises.notes: Hold the top position of a hanging leg raise for the time interval given. Aim to increase the hold time week to week.

exercises.name: Omni-grip lat pulldown
exercises.notes: 1 set wide grip (overhand), 1 set middle grip (overhand), 1 set close grip (underhand)

exercises.name: Barbell overhead press
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes to keep your torso upright, press up and slightly back

exercises.name: Chest-supported row
exercises.notes: Can use machine or dumbbells. Full stretch at the bottom, squeeze at the top

exercises.name: Close-grip bench press
exercises.notes: Tuck your elbows against your sides more than you would on standard grip bench press

exercises.name: Seated face pull
exercises.notes: Mind muscle connection with rear delts on set 1 (sweep the weight out). Mind muscle connection with mid-traps on set 2 (squeeze shoulder blades hard).

exercises.name: Dumbbell lateral raise 21s
exercises.notes: First 7 reps: top half of ROM, middle 7 reps: full ROM, last 7 reps: bottom half of ROM

exercises.name: Incline dumbbell curl
exercises.notes: Keep your elbows locked in place to maintain a stretch on the biceps

exercises.name: Neck flexion/extension (optional)
exercises.notes: 10 reps flexion (front of neck), 10 reps extension (back of neck)

exercises.notes: Top set, get comfortable with heavier loads while keeping perfect technique

exercises.notes: If you low bar squat, do front squat. If you high bar squat, do barbell box squat

exercises.notes: Keep your hips straight, do Nordic ham curls if no GHR machine

exercises.name: A2: Prisoner back extension
exercises.notes: Place your hands behind your head and squeeze your glutes to straighen your hips

exercises.name: Unilateral standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Start with your weaker side. Think about rolling back and forth on the balls of your feet

exercises.name: L-sit hold
exercises.notes: Hold the top position of a hanging leg raise for the time interval given. Aim to increase the hold time week to week.

exercises.name: Omni-grip lat pulldown
exercises.notes: 1 set wide grip (overhand), 1 set middle grip (overhand), 1 set close grip (underhand)

exercises.name: Barbell overhead press
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes to keep your torso upright, press up and slightly back

exercises.name: Chest-supported row
exercises.notes: Can use machine or dumbbells. Full stretch at the bottom, squeeze at the top

exercises.name: Close-grip bench press
exercises.notes: Tuck your elbows against your sides more than you would on standard grip bench press

exercises.name: Seated face pull
exercises.notes: Mind muscle connection with rear delts on set 1 (sweep the weight out). Mind muscle connection with mid-traps on set 2 (squeeze shoulder blades hard).

exercises.name: Dumbbell lateral raise 21s
exercises.notes: First 7 reps: top half of ROM, middle 7 reps: full ROM, last 7 reps: bottom half of ROM

exercises.name: Incline dumbbell curl
exercises.notes: Keep your elbows locked in place to maintain a stretch on the biceps

exercises.name: Neck flexion/extension (optional)
exercises.notes: 10 reps flexion (front of neck), 10 reps extension (back of neck)

exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.notes: Maintain tight pressure in your upper back against the bar

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Set up a comfortable arch, quick pause on the chest and explode up on each rep

exercises.name: Wide-grip lat pulldown
exercises.notes: 1.5x shoulder width grip. Think about pulling your elbows "down" and "in"

exercises.name: Sliding leg curl
exercises.notes: Keep your hips high, think about "pulling your heels into your hips"

exercises.name: Wall slide
exercises.notes: Don't use weight. These will help with shoulder stability. Don't skip!

exercises.name: Opposite stance deadlift
exercises.notes: If you normally perform deadlifts sumo, perform conventional, and vice versa. Use your opposite stance estimated max.

exercises.name: Barbell overhead press
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes to keep your torso upright, press up and slightly back

exercises.name: Leg press
exercises.notes: If you low bar squat, use a low foot placement. If you high bar squat, use a high foot placement

exercises.name: Seated cable row
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together, drive your elbows down and back

exercises.name: Hammer curl
exercises.notes: Go heavy, use a tiny bit of momentum

exercises.name: Barbell or dumbbell isometric hold (grip work)
exercises.notes: Do an isometric hold with dumbbells or a barbell

exercises.name: Front squat
exercises.notes: Try adding weight to the load you used in Week 5

exercises.name: Pause barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Get comfortable pausing with heavy weight, 2-3 second pause

exercises.name: Weighted neutral-grip pull-up
exercises.notes: Pull your chest to the bar, add weight if needed to hit RPE

exercises.name: Leg curl (choice)
exercises.notes: Use seated leg curl if available. Can use lying leg curl or Nordic ham curl if no machine access. Focus on the mind-muscle connection

exercises.name: Prone trap raise
exercises.notes: Think about tucking your shoulder blades "down" as you raise your arms. Mind muscle connection with mid-lower traps

exercises.name: Hanging leg raise
exercises.notes: Knees to chest, controlled reps, straighten legs more to increase difficulty

exercises.name: Standing calf raise
exercises.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep, full squeeze at the top

exercises.name: 1" Block pull
exercises.notes: These will start to feel very heavy. Only do 1 set if you're feeling very fatigued from it

exercises.name: Dip
exercises.notes: Add weight or assistance as needed. Do DB floor press if no access to dip handles.

exercises.name: One-arm row
exercises.notes: Can use DB or cable. Minimize torso momentum.

exercises.name: Triceps pressdown 21s
exercises.notes: First 7 reps: full ROM, next 7 reps: bottom half of ROM, last 7 reps: top half of ROM

exercises.name: DB lateral raise
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your delts

exercises.name: Pin squat
exercises.notes: Set the pins to just above parallel

exercises.name: Barbell RDL
exercises.notes: Emphasize the stretch in your hamstrings, prevent your lower back from rounding

exercises.name: A1: Sissy squat
exercises.notes: Or leg press with low foot placement. Let your knees travel forward. Mind muscle connection with quads.

exercises.name: A2: Nordic ham curl
exercises.notes: Keep your hips as straight as you can, can sub for lying leg curl

exercises.name: Unilateral standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Start with your weaker side. Think about rolling back and forth on the balls of your feet

exercises.name: Hip abduction
exercises.notes: Machine, band, or weighted, 1 second isometric hold at the top of each rep

exercises.name: A1: Cable crunch (abs)
exercises.notes: Squeeze your six pack to crunch the weight, don't yank with your hands

exercises.name: A2: Cable shrug-in (traps)
exercises.notes: Set up two cable handles low and shrug up and in. Squeeze your upper traps to move the weight: https://youtu.be/C6sYjDFuq9I?t=357

exercises.name: Larsen press
exercises.notes: Shoulder blades still retracted and depressed. Slight arch in upper back. Zero leg drive.

exercises.name: Machine chest-supported row
exercises.notes: Dropset on the last set

exercises.name: Machine incline press
exercises.notes: Smith machine or hammer strength machine. Use incline DB press if no machine access. ~45 degree incline.

exercises.name: Single-arm pulldown
exercises.notes: Start with your weaker side

exercises.name: Triceps pressdown
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your triceps

exercises.name: Inverse Zottman curl
exercises.notes: Hammer curl on concentric, supinated curl (palms up) on the eccentric

exercises.name: Lateral raise (choice)
exercises.notes: Can use cable, dumbbell or bands. Use what you 'feel' the most. Stay in constant tension.

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.notes: These are intentionally light. Lock in your technique and move the bar with max speed

exercises.name: Hack squat
exercises.notes: Allow your knees to come forward (past your toes), focus the tension on your quads

exercises.name: Cable pull-through
exercises.notes: Contract your glutes hard at the top, don't allow your lower back to round

exercises.name: Leg extension
exercises.notes: Dropset. Do 15 reps, drop the weight up to 50%, do another 10 reps.

exercises.name: Unilateral leg curl (choice)
exercises.notes: Can perform seated or lying. Focus on contracting your hamstrings

exercises.name: Standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Think about rolling back and forth on the balls of your feet

exercises.name: L-sit hold
exercises.notes: Hold the top position of a hanging leg raise for the time interval given. Aim to increase the hold time week to week.

exercises.name: Weighted eccentric-overload pull-up
exercises.notes: Jump up to assist with the positive or use a partner to help you on the positive, then control the negative for 5 seconds.

exercises.name: Eccentric-accentuated pull-up
exercises.notes: 3-second lowering phase. Use assistance/resistance as needed

exercises.name: Barbell overhead press
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes to keep your torso upright, press up and slightly back

exercises.name: Pendlay row / bent over row
exercises.notes: First 5 reps: very strict Pendlay rows. Final 10 reps: "cheat" barbell bent over rows (use controlled momentum and pull to stomach)

exercises.name: Deficit push-up
exercises.notes: 4" deficit. Sink your chest deep. Track your reps for next week.

exercises.name: Barbell or EZ bar curl
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your biceps, minimize torso momentum

exercises.name: Dumbbell lateral raise iso-hold
exercises.notes: Hold the dumbbell with your arms parallel to the floor for the time specified

exercises.name: Back squat
exercises.notes: Maintain tight pressure in your upper back against the bar

exercises.name: Barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Set up a comfortable arch, quick pause on the chest and explode up on each rep

exercises.name: Wide-grip lat pulldown
exercises.notes: 1.5x shoulder width grip. Think about pulling your elbows "down" and "in"

exercises.name: Sliding leg curl
exercises.notes: Keep your hips high, think about "pulling your heels into your hips"

exercises.name: Wall slide
exercises.notes: Don't use weight. These will help with shoulder stability. Don't skip!

exercises.name: Opposite stance deadlift
exercises.notes: If you normally perform deadlifts sumo, perform conventional, and vice versa. Use your opposite stance estimated max. Heavy deadlifts in a couple days, so go lighter on the weight

exercises.name: Barbell overhead press
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes to keep your torso upright, press up and slightly back

exercises.name: Leg press
exercises.notes: If you low bar squat, use a low foot placement. If you high bar squat, use a high foot placement

exercises.name: Seated cable row
exercises.notes: Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together, drive your elbows down and back

exercises.name: Hammer curl
exercises.notes: Go heavy, use a tiny bit of momentum

exercises.name: Barbell or dumbbell isometric hold (grip work)
exercises.notes: Do an isometric hold with dumbbells or a barbell

exercises.name: Front squat
exercises.notes: Stay light, keep your torso upright

exercises.name: Pause barbell bench press
exercises.notes: Get comfortable pausing with heavy weight, 2-3 second pause

exercises.name: Weighted neutral-grip pull-up
exercises.notes: Pull your chest to the bar, add weight if needed to hit RPE

exercises.name: Leg curl (choice)
exercises.notes: Use seated leg curl if available. Can use lying leg curl or Nordic ham curl if no machine access. Focus on the mind-muscle connection

exercises.name: Prone trap raise
exercises.notes: Think about tucking your shoulder blades "down" as you raise your arms. Mind muscle connection with mid-lower traps

exercises.name: Hanging leg raise
exercises.notes: Knees to chest, controlled reps, straighten legs more to increase difficulty

exercises.name: Standing calf raise
exercises.notes: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep, full squeeze at the top

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.notes: Aim for a PR for 3-6 reps

exercises.name: Dip
exercises.notes: Add weight or assistance as needed. Do DB floor press if no access to dip handles.

exercises.name: One-arm row
exercises.notes: Can use DB or cable. Minimize torso momentum.

exercises.name: Triceps pressdown 21s
exercises.notes: First 7 reps: full ROM, next 7 reps: bottom half of ROM, last 7 reps: top half of ROM

exercises.name: DB lateral raise
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your delts

exercises.name: Pin squat
exercises.notes: Set the pins to just above parallel

exercises.name: Barbell RDL
exercises.notes: Emphasize the stretch in your hamstrings, prevent your lower back from rounding

exercises.name: A1: Sissy squat
exercises.notes: Or leg press with low foot placement. Let your knees travel forward. Mind muscle connection with quads.

exercises.name: A2: Nordic ham curl
exercises.notes: Keep your hips as straight as you can, can sub for lying leg curl

exercises.name: Unilateral standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Start with your weaker side. Think about rolling back and forth on the balls of your feet

exercises.name: Hip abduction
exercises.notes: Machine, band, or weighted, 1 second isometric hold at the top of each rep

exercises.name: A1: Cable crunch (abs)
exercises.notes: Squeeze your six pack to crunch the weight, don't yank with your hands

exercises.name: A2: Cable shrug-in (traps)
exercises.notes: Set up two cable handles low and shrug up and in. Squeeze your upper traps to move the weight: https://youtu.be/C6sYjDFuq9I?t=357

exercises.name: Larsen press
exercises.notes: Shoulder blades still retracted and depressed. Slight arch in upper back. Zero leg drive.

exercises.name: Machine chest-supported row
exercises.notes: Dropset on the last set

exercises.name: Machine incline press
exercises.notes: Smith machine or hammer strength machine. Use incline DB press if no machine access. ~45 degree incline.

exercises.name: Single-arm pulldown
exercises.notes: Start with your weaker side

exercises.name: Triceps pressdown
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your triceps

exercises.name: Inverse Zottman curl
exercises.notes: Hammer curl on concentric, supinated curl (palms up) on the eccentric

exercises.name: Lateral raise (choice)
exercises.notes: Can use cable, dumbbell or bands. Use what you 'feel' the most. Stay in constant tension.

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.notes: Use this as a time to perfect your form

exercises.name: Hack squat
exercises.notes: Allow your knees to come forward (past your toes), focus the tension on your quads

exercises.name: Cable pull-through
exercises.notes: Contract your glutes hard at the top, don't allow your lower back to round

exercises.name: Leg extension
exercises.notes: Dropset. Do 15 reps, drop the weight up to 50%, do another 10 reps.

exercises.name: Unilateral leg curl (choice)
exercises.notes: Can perform seated or lying. Focus on contracting your hamstrings

exercises.name: Standing calf raise
exercises.notes: Think about rolling back and forth on the balls of your feet

exercises.name: Barbell bench press (RPE 9.5 AMRAP)
exercises.notes: Do as many reps as possible to an RPE 9-9.5. Don't actually fail. Use a spotter

exercises.name: Barbell bench press (Back off)
exercises.notes: Go lighter, flare your elbows slightly more than normal

exercises.name: Machine chest-supported row
exercises.notes: Dropset on the last set

exercises.name: L-sit hold

exercises.name: Weighted eccentric-overload pull-up
exercises.notes: Jump up to assist with the positive or use a partner to help you on the positive, then control the negative for 5 seconds.

exercises.name: Eccentric-accentuated pull-up
exercises.notes: 3-second lowering phase. Use assistance/resistance as needed

exercises.name: Barbell overhead press
exercises.notes: Squeeze your glutes to keep your torso upright, press up and slightly back

exercises.name: Pendlay row / bent over row
exercises.notes: First 5 reps: very strict Pendlay rows. Final 10 reps: "cheat" barbell bent over rows (use controlled momentum and pull to stomach)

exercises.name: Deficit push-up
exercises.notes: 4" deficit. Sink your chest deep. Beat your reps from last week.

exercises.name: Barbell or EZ bar curl
exercises.notes: Focus on contracting your biceps, minimize torso momentum

exercises.name: Dumbbell lateral raise iso-hold
exercises.notes: Hold the dumbbell with your arms parallel to the floor for the time specified

exercises.name: A1: Hammer curl
exercises.notes: Do both arms at once. Use a small amount of momentum, control the eccentric

exercises.name: A2: Single-arm overhead triceps extension
exercises.notes: Can use cable, band or dumbbell. Focus on stretching the triceps, keep your elbow locked in place

exercises.name: B1: Cable curl
exercises.notes: Traditional cable curl using a bar attachment. Keep the cable in motion, no pausing at the bottom or top

exercises.name: B2: Cable triceps kickback
exercises.notes: Lean slightly forward, lock your elbow behind your torso (shoulder hyperextension)

exercises.name: Enhanced-eccentric calf raise
exercises.notes: Do the concentric with both legs, then do the eccentric unilaterally with a 3-second lowering phase

exercises.name: C1: Plate shrug
exercises.notes: Brief pause at the top and bottom of ROM. Think about pulling your shoulders up to your ears!

exercises.name: C2: Neck flexion/extension (optional)
exercises.notes: 15 reps flexion (front of neck), 15 reps extension (back of neck)
